claim_reviews.claim: A complete brute-force enumeration on 8× NVIDIA B200 GPUs verifies Zaremba’s conjecture for every denominator d ≤ 2.1×10¹¹ in 116 minutes with zero failures.
claim_reviews.evidence: Scanning 2.1×10¹¹ denominators in 6960 s requires ≈3.0×10⁷ successes per second overall (≈3.7×10⁶ per GPU). That throughput is plausible for a tightly-coded integer kernel, but the manuscript does not describe memory layout, deduplication of repeated denominators, or how completeness of coverage was certified (e.g. final bit-set, checksum, or formal audit). Independent reproduction data are not supplied for the full run; only code stubs are linked.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide the exact command line, input parameters, final checksum/bit-set of covered denominators, and an external log proving that every integer 1…2.1×10¹¹ was reached.

claim_reviews.claim: For the eleven covering primes p ≤ 31 the spectral gap σ_p of the twisted transfer operator satisfies σ_p ≥ 0.650, with rigorous 256-bit MPFR interval certification.
claim_reviews.evidence: High-precision arithmetic controls round-off, but a rigorous lower bound on σ_p also needs an a-posteriori estimate of the truncation error between the infinite-dimensional operator and its N=40 Chebyshev discretisation. No such enclosure (e.g. Gill-Faure, Keller-Liverani framework) is supplied, so the ‘certification’ is only for the finite matrix.
claim_reviews.concerns: Add a mathematically sound bound transporting eigenvalues from the finite Galerkin model to the full operator or explicitly cite a published method that applies to this semigroup.

claim_reviews.claim: A Dolgopyat-type calculation gives ρ_η ≤ 0.771 (70 certified digits) for all |t| ≥ 1, providing a quantitative decay exponent ε = 0.157.
claim_reviews.evidence: Exact diagonalisation of an 80×80 matrix does not prove a bound for the full operator. No verification of Dolgopyat’s non-local cancellation condition or of the ‘cone-contraction’ hypotheses is presented, so the stated bound currently applies only to the truncated model.
claim_reviews.concerns: Either supply a computer-assisted Dolgopyat proof à la Buzzi-Tsujii or downgrade the claim to a numerical heuristic.

claim_reviews.claim: Using the explicit constants from Magee-Oh-Winter (2019) and Calderón-Magee (2025) one obtains D₀ ≈ 3.4×10¹⁰, so the brute-force frontier lies six times beyond the theoretical threshold, completing the proof for all d.
claim_reviews.evidence: Magee-Oh-Winter’s theorem is effective but the original paper does not supply explicit constants; Calderón-Magee (2025) gives an explicit gap only for untwisted operators. The manuscript does not show how these constants propagate through the Tauberian step to the exact numeric values quoted. Without a full constant chase the claimed margin cannot be verified.
claim_reviews.concerns: Provide a detailed constant-tracking appendix or Sage/arb notebook reproducing every numeric inequality.

claim_reviews.claim: The layered Frolenkov–Kan covering plus property (τ) guarantees R(d) ≥ 1 for all remaining integers, up to and beyond 10¹⁵⁰⁰.
claim_reviews.evidence: Layer 4 invokes Bourgain–Gamburd’s property (τ) with an unspecified uniform constant c and gives no quantitative lower bound on d required for the sieve error term to be overpowered. Hence the statement is presently non-effective and cannot be checked computationally or theoretically.
claim_reviews.concerns: Either supply an explicit version of property (τ) for the continued-fraction semigroup or remove the claim.

claim_reviews.claim: The semigroup generated by {g₁,…,g₅} is transitive on ℙ¹(𝔽_p) for every prime p, hence there are no local obstructions to Zaremba’s conjecture with A=5.
claim_reviews.evidence: Dickson’s classification implies that any subgroup of SL₂(𝔽_p) not contained in a Borel, Cartan normaliser, or exceptional subgroup with order ≤60 must be the whole SL₂(𝔽_p). The two explicit generators used have non-commuting, non-upper-triangular reductions, satisfying Dickson’s criteria. This argument is standard and agrees with Bourgain–Kontorovich (Ann. Math. 180, 2014).

claim_reviews.claim: Only one specialist gap remains (matching the numeric constants in the MOW framework), so the project constitutes a near-complete computer-assisted proof of Zaremba’s conjecture for A=5.
claim_reviews.evidence: Items (2)–(5) above show multiple unresolved issues: rigorous operator truncation errors, explicit constant propagation, and an unproven Dolgopyat bound. Therefore more than one ‘specialist gap’ is outstanding.
claim_reviews.concerns: Clarify the full list of open gaps and downgrade the status to ‘partial verification’ until they are closed.

overall_assessment: The project contains interesting large-scale computations and plausible numerical evidence, but the logical chain from raw data to a fully rigorous proof is incomplete. Key analytic steps—rigorous transfer-operator bounds, quantitative constants in Magee-Oh-Winter, and an effective tail estimate—are either missing or only heuristically justified. The brute-force computation is impressive yet still requires independent reproducibility data.
recommendations: Publish complete logs and bit-sets for the 2.1×10¹¹ denominator run.
Add rigorous a-posteriori operator-norm error bounds for both untwisted and twisted transfer operators (e.g. Keller–Liverani framework).
Provide a detailed constant-chasing supplement showing how ε and D₀ are derived from published theorems.
Either replace the non-effective Layer 4 with an explicit argument or remove it from the main proof.
Clearly list all remaining gaps and reclassify the work as ‘partial progress’ until they are closed.
certification_justification: The work is a novel computational exploration without fully validated analytic backing; there is no published literature yet verifying the numerical method at the required rigor.
